external_narratives.summary: The American Air Museum records B-24J 42-50452 'Earthquake McGoon' of the 566th Bomb Squadron, 389th Bomb Group based at Hethel. The aircraft collided with 44-10513 'Old Glory' during formation assembly on 21 November 1944. Pilot Lt Alfred Brooks was the sole survivor from this aircraft, with 17 total killed across both planes.
key_details: Pilot Lt Alfred Brooks was sole survivor from this aircraft
Memorial board at All Saints Church, Carleton Rode

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Photon geocoder — Carleton Rode village NR16 1RL at 52.4913, 1.1114

location_confidence.verification_notes: INACCURATE. Previous coords (52.807, 0.950) ~36km too far NW near Fakenham/Guist. Same mid-air collision as carleton-rode-1 on 21-Nov-44 — both aircraft came down in same general area. Village centroid used at 52.491, 1.111.
